title: Second CySA+ Practice Set
summary: 20 additional CySA+ practice questions and 5 SOC scenarios focused on identity attacks, memory-resident malware, insider threat, web compromise, and SOC operations.

questions.question:
A SOC analyst notices that endpoint logs and firewall logs differ by approximately 52 minutes during an investigation.

Which of the following is the MOST likely explanation?
questions.myAnswer: C, because NTP is responsible for synchronizing time

questions.question:
An EDR alert identifies suspicious activity from:
svch0st.exe

Which of the following would BEST help verify whether the file is malicious?
questions.myAnswer: I think A, because other variants are not helpful in this situation

questions.question:
An organization wants the MOST trustworthy source of intelligence regarding active ransomware campaigns targeting healthcare infrastructure.

Which source is BEST?
questions.myAnswer: B, because they are closest to real info about real threats, especially healthcare sector.

questions.question:
A SOC team receives thousands of repetitive low-value alerts daily.

Which solution would MOST effectively automate duplicate alert handling?
questions.myAnswer: A, because SOAR automates the whole detection process.

questions.question:
A legacy industrial control system cannot be patched because updates would interrupt manufacturing operations.

Which control BEST reduces organizational risk?
questions.myAnswer: B, because if system can't be patched, it should be as hard to access for attackers as possible

questions.question: Which framework is MOST useful for mapping attacker behavior and TTPs?
questions.myAnswer: B

questions.question:
A public-facing Linux web server is actively encrypting files during a ransomware attack.

What should be done FIRST?
questions.myAnswer: B, to stop spreading to other systems and minimize damage

questions.question: During which incident response phase is root cause analysis MOST likely performed?
questions.myAnswer: B, because during detection and analysis finding out what is the root cause of attack can help a lot in containment and eradication

questions.question: After a major security incident concludes, which activity BEST improves future response capabilities?
questions.myAnswer: A, because next time IR team would have more experience and knowledge

questions.question:
An analyst has removed malware persistence mechanisms and deleted malicious files from a compromised endpoint.

Which incident response phase does this represent?
questions.myAnswer: D

questions.question:
A cloud administrator receives repeated MFA push notifications without attempting login.

Which attack is MOST likely occurring?
questions.myAnswer: A, because in this attack attackers goal is to make victim tired and overwhelmed by MFA requests so victim just accepts them

questions.question:
A security analyst wants to identify malicious activity that exists only in volatile memory.

Which action is BEST?
questions.myAnswer: A, because RAM hold info until power is turned off.

questions.question: Which technology BEST helps identify abnormal user behavior over time?
questions.myAnswer: B, because UEBA uses pattern recognition over time for specific user

questions.question:
An analyst notices OAuth tokens being used from unusual geographic locations after a phishing campaign.

What is the MOST likely concern?
questions.myAnswer: A, because attacker gained access to Tokens/session and is using them

questions.question:
A SIEM detects:
- PowerShell launched from Excel
- External HTTPS connection
- Registry Run key creation

What is the MOST likely attacker objective?
questions.myAnswer: A, because connecting to external https, creating registry run keys and launching powershell from weird places are typical indicators of persistence and C2 phase

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes a compensating control?
options: A different security measure reducing risk when primary mitigation is unavailable
questions.myAnswer: B, because compensating control is control where its impossible to fully fix vulnerability or patch system so defender just compensates it, trying to minimize the damage in other ways.

questions.question:
A company wants to safely analyze suspicious malware without risking production systems.

Which solution is BEST?
questions.myAnswer: A, because sandboxes are made to be isolated and safe.

questions.question:
An analyst notices a large volume of outbound encrypted traffic to a domain registered two days ago.

What should be investigated FIRST?
questions.myAnswer: A, because traffic to newly registered domains is typical for data exfil

questions.question:
A host repeatedly reconnects externally after reboot despite malware removal attempts.

Which is the MOST likely explanation?
questions.myAnswer: A, because after removal attempts of malware attacker still has persistence in machine

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes threat hunting?
options: Proactively searching for indicators of compromise
questions.myAnswer: B, threat hunting is basically searching/hunting for threats, instead of passively waiting

scenarios.title: Microsoft 365 OAuth Token Abuse

sections.title: Microsoft 365 Alerts
items: Multiple successful logins from different countries within 15 minutes

sections.title: Additional Findings
items: OAuth token remains active after password reset

tasks: What MOST likely occurred?
Why did password reset not fully solve the issue?
What should be revoked FIRST?
What logs would help investigation most?
What controls could reduce similar attacks?

myAnswers.answer: Token/session hijack, because attacker can bypass MFA by having tokens of authenticated user
Because attacker has access to account/session, so resetting password isn't short term fix
Lower "Mail-backup-sync" accounts privileges or disable it fully until deeper investigation
IP addresses of logins, OAUTH access granting service logs
UEBA by checking unusual country logins, phishing training campaign

scenarios.title: Memory-Resident Malware

sections.title: EDR Alerts
items: No malicious files found on disk

tasks: What type of malware behavior is MOST likely occurring?
Why might antivirus fail to detect it?
What forensic evidence should be collected FIRST?
What containment action is MOST appropriate?
What indicators strongly suggest memory-resident malware?

myAnswers.answer: Malware living in RAM and doing data exfil
Because antivirus doesn't scan RAM directly
Memory dump, suspicious traffic
Isolate machine from internet/network
RAM usage high, malware disappearing, no malicious files found on disk

scenarios.title: Insider Threat Data Exfiltration

sections.title: DLP Alerts
items: Files uploaded to personal cloud storage

sections.title: Additional Findings
items: Employee submitted resignation two days ago
VPN access originated from normal geographic location

tasks: What threat scenario is MOST likely?
What should analysts verify FIRST?
What evidence would strengthen insider threat suspicion?
What containment actions are appropriate?
How should this incident severity be classified?

myAnswers.answer: Insider threat, employee might be threat doing data exfil or spying
Employees identity, then any suspicion on being insider threat
Recent resignation submition, weird activity timing, and possible sensitive data downloaded
Lock account, revoke access
High, because of possible sensitive data exfil

scenarios.title: Web Shell & Server Compromise

sections.title: Additional Findings
items: Outbound HTTPS traffic to VPS provider
IIS logs show repeated POST requests

tasks: What attack behavior is MOST likely occurring?
What persistence indicators exist?
What should be isolated FIRST?
What forensic artifacts would be valuable?
Which MITRE ATT&CK techniques are likely involved?

myAnswers.answer: Reverse shell execution through web server, then priv esc (new admin account created), then data exfil
Outbound traffic, new local admin account
Affected server/machine, then admin acc
POST requests, cmd execution, outbound traffic

scenarios.title: SOC Alert Fatigue
tasks: What is the PRIMARY operational problem?
Which technologies/processes could improve efficiency?
What risks does alert fatigue create?
What metrics should leadership monitor?
What SIEM improvements would MOST reduce noise?

myAnswers.answer: Un-tuned detection logic, too much manual work
SIEM/SOAR
Missed true positives, analysts burnout
Missed true postiives, analysts well being, increasing response time weekly
Tuning and specifying detection logic to reduce false positives and reduce manual work
